commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4d7dfe4d1cdee425facfc862cccc5f5ac8d02963)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Codex app-server resume check that projects mirrored OpenClaw history when user-visible transcript messages are newer than the saved thread binding, plus regression coverage for that path.

PR surface: Source +30, Tests +40. Total +70 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: binding updatedAt is written on resume before mirroring, and Codex’s own mirrored user/assistant messages are later read as history after that timestamp on the next run.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor repair plus real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue an automated repair while the external proof gate is unsatisfied.

target.title: fix(codex): project newer history on app-server resume
target.body:
## Summary
- project mirrored OpenClaw chat history when a Codex app-server thread is resumed and the saved binding is older than user-visible transcript messages
- keep existing native Codex thread ownership and only bridge newer OpenClaw context into the resumed turn input
- add regression coverage for an existing binding that resumes but still receives prior chat context in `turn/start`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: resumed Codex app-server turns no longer send only the bare current prompt when the OpenClaw session transcript has newer user-visible chat history than the saved Codex thread binding.
Real environment tested: Codex app-server harness in the OpenClaw extension test runner, using a real session JSONL file, a persisted Codex app-server binding, and the app-server `thread/resume` plus `turn/start` request flow.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: the new regression test writes an existing Codex thread binding, appends newer mirrored user and assistant messages, resumes the thread, and verifies the emitted `turn/start` input contains `OpenClaw assembled context for this turn:`, the prior Sonnet-leak context, the prior David Ondrej assistant context, and the current request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed with 2 files and 236 tests; the resumed existing-binding path used `thread/resume` and still projected newer OpenClaw chat history into the Codex input.
What was not tested: a live Telegram DM with a real Codex OAuth app-server; this PR proves the source-level resumed-binding path that ClawSweeper identified, but it does not exercise Telegram transport or a live OpenAI/Codex account.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

Fixes #86449

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>
target.author: turbotheturtle
